data.title: Colombia Orders Crypto Exchanges to Report User Data in Tax Crackdown — Here’s What’s at Stake
data.description:
Under Resolution 000240, Colombia’s tax authority requires crypto exchanges and intermediaries to collect and submit detailed user and transaction data, aligning with international standards to combat tax evasion and improve transparency in the local digital asset sector.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/colombia-crypto-exchanges-report-user-data-tax-crackdown/

data.title: Masked Gunmen Steal Crypto USB in France as Tax Official Caught Selling Data to Gangs
data.description:
Three masked individuals broke into a Manosque home on January 6 and seized a crypto USB drive after threatening the victim at gunpoint, as French prosecutors charged tax official Ghalia C. with accessing government databases to provide crypto investor addresses to organized crime for roughly 800 euros per operation amid 65 documented global attacks in 2025.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/masked-gunmen-steal-crypto-usb-in-france-as-tax-official-caught-selling-data-to-gangs/

data.title: A whale holding $790 million in positions in 1011 that opened short positions after the flash crash is currently experiencing a floating loss of $6.4 million, with funding fees already exceeding $5.4 million.
data.description: According to AiYi's monitoring, the whale (0xb31...83ae) that shorted after the 1011 flash crash currently has a total holding value of approximately $790 million, with an overall unrealized loss of $6.4 million and over $5.4 million in funding fees paid. The specific holdings and profit/loss details of this address are as follows: ETH: 203,340.64 ETH, valued at $630 million, opening price $3147.39, unrealized loss of $9.8 million; BTC: 1000 BTC, valued at $90.55 million, opening price $91,506.7, unrealized loss of $957,000; SOL: 511,000 BTC, valued at $71.01 million, opening price $130.1911, unrealized profit of $4.41 million.

data.title: After two years of inactivity, a major whale deposited $2.64 million USDC into Hyperliquid and purchased 59,976 HYPE tokens.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale (0x3a1...48c9) deposited $2.64 million USDC into Hyperliquid for the first time in two years and spent $1.54 million to buy 59,976 HYPE tokens. The whale currently holds $1.09 million USDC and has open orders to continue buying.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Reflects on Investment Challenges and Missed Opportunities
data.description:
According to PANews, Liquid Capital founder Jack Yi shared insights on the X platform about the complexities of investment, emphasizing the importance of patience in achieving successful outcomes. Yi highlighted that while timing the market is crucial, enduring the waiting period is equally significant. He recounted his team's experience with Solana (SOL), noting that they published a research report on July 26, 2023, and began accumulating SOL around the $20 mark, believing in the undervalued potential of its ecosystem.

However, Yi explained that subsequent news regarding FTX's OTC shares led to panic, prompting them to liquidate their holdings below $50, thereby missing out on a subsequent 15-fold increase in value. He remarked on the deceptive simplicity of investment and trading, pointing out that the process from initiation to outcome is the most challenging aspect, posing a significant test against human nature. Yi concluded by acknowledging the unpredictability of short-term market fluctuations and the duration of the waiting period, noting that apart from vocal critics and analysts who claim to predict market movements accurately, no one can truly foresee these dynamics.

data.title: Large Transfer of DOGE Observed Between Anonymous Addresses
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 18:52, a total of 85,343,100 DOGE was transferred from one anonymous address, starting with DR1DUh, to another anonymous address, beginning with DJGBi3.

data.title: Uniswap Achieves Record Daily Fee Revenue
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Dune indicates that Uniswap has reached a new peak in daily fee revenue, capturing approximately $1.4 million. A significant portion of this, around $1.3 million, was contributed by the Truebit Protocol token TRU, which was recently targeted in a hacking incident.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Index Surpasses 99 for First Time Since December
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on January 9, the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) exceeded 99, marking the first time it has reached this level since December 10 of the previous year. The index saw a daily increase of 0.14%.

data.title: Binance Expands Blockchain Yatra to Bengaluru Amid India Blockchain Week 2025
data.description:
Binance Blog published a new article, highlighting the expansion of Binance's multi-city Blockchain Yatra to Bengaluru, marking the fifth stop in its Web3 education tour across India. This edition coincided with India Blockchain Week 2025, fostering a week of community engagement among developers, founders, and students. The initiative aims to engage India's Web3 community through talks, panels, and in-person sessions focused on responsible blockchain innovation.

India's Web3 landscape is rapidly evolving, with over 1,200 Web3 startups and nearly 12% of the global blockchain developer pool. Karnataka, with Bengaluru as its hub, accounts for about one-third of the country's Web3 ecosystem, supported by 18,000 startups and a dense concentration of engineering and product talent. The recently approved Karnataka Startup Policy 2025-2030, with a ₹518 crore (approximately 62 million USD) allocation, aims to foster 25,000 deep-tech startups, further boosting the region's innovation ecosystem.

During India Blockchain Week 2025, Binance representatives participated in keynotes, panels, and community engagements, focusing on responsible blockchain innovation and practical adoption across the region. SB Seker, Head of APAC at Binance, emphasized the region's shift toward infrastructure-led adoption, noting India's potential as a builder market. He highlighted the country's deep talent pool, strong digital foundation, and emerging on-chain tokenization initiatives as key advantages. Seker stated that if regulation and innovation align, India could lead in defining responsible blockchain adoption, with Binance supporting this journey through education, technology, and governance.

Seker also joined a panel titled "The Role of Crypto Platforms in Empowering Local Innovation," discussing developer-led innovation, tokenization opportunities, and the shift toward regulated, infrastructure-driven adoption across APAC. He emphasized Binance's focus on education, talent enablement, and real-world blockchain applications across India.

The Bengaluru edition of Blockchain Yatra brought together students, developers, founders, and Web3 enthusiasts, building on the success of previous stops in Visakhapatnam, Ahmedabad, Chennai, and Mumbai. The event focused on direct engagement with builders and students. Seker explained that Bengaluru's vibrant developer ecosystem and entrepreneurial spirit make it an ideal venue for the Blockchain Yatra. Aligning the event with India Blockchain Week allowed for deep community connections, reinforcing Binance's commitment to nurturing India's Web3 ecosystem through education and collaboration.

The Bengaluru edition of Binance Blockchain Yatra facilitated in-depth conversations about building in Web3. As the tour continues across India, the focus remains on practical education, direct connection, and clearer pathways for the next wave of talent to learn, grow, and contribute to the blockchain ecosystem.

data.title: Kalshi's January Trading Volume Projected to Reach $8.4 Billion
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, data released by KalshiData indicates that as of January 6, Kalshi's monthly nominal trading volume is approximately $1.6 billion, averaging around $272 million per day. If this pace continues, Kalshi anticipates that January's nominal trading volume will reach about $8.4 billion.

Previous data showed that in December 2025, Kalshi's trading volume was $6.38 billion, marking a record high for monthly trading volume.

data.title: BitMart launches DeepNode (DN) USDT trading pair
data.description: According to an official announcement, BitMart will list DeepNode (DN) on January 9th at 19:00 (UTC+8), with the DN/USDT trading pair available. DeepNode is a decentralized AI infrastructure network that enables developers, validators, miners, and businesses to collaborate on building, deploying, and commercializing AI models across various industries. The network operates using a proprietary Proof-of-Work Relevance (PoWR) consensus mechanism, which rewards contributors based on actual utility rather than speculative behavior.

data.title: Two new wallets spent $23,000 betting on an Israeli attack on Iran before January 31.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, two newly created wallets named goldbless (0x3e5...a908) and Getthegoop (0xa06...e5a9) spent $23,000 betting that "Israel will attack Iran before January 31, 2026." The purchase price was 0.36 USDT.

data.title: Bitcoin Staking Protocol Babylon Faces Potential Vulnerability
data.description:
According to PANews, a software vulnerability in the Bitcoin staking protocol Babylon has been disclosed, potentially allowing malicious validators to disrupt part of the network's consensus process. This issue, revealed by developers in a GitHub post on Thursday, could slow down block generation during critical periods.

The vulnerability affects Babylon's block signature scheme, known as the BLS voting extension scheme, which is used to confirm that validators have reached consensus on a block. Malicious validators could exploit this flaw by omitting the block hash field when sending vote extensions, leading to consensus issues among validators at network epoch boundaries.

The block hash field is crucial as it informs validators about the specific blocks they are supporting during the consensus process. By omitting this field, malicious validators could theoretically cause other validators to crash during critical consensus checks at phase boundaries. If multiple validators are affected, it could result in a slowdown of block generation.

While there have been no reports of this vulnerability being actively exploited, developers have warned that it could be abused if not addressed.
